---
title: "3RM -- INFORM Update"
jupyter: ds-storm-impact
---

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
## Summary

The [Rost (2025)](https://zenodo.org/records/15164535) 3RM model uses CIRV
(CERF Index for Risk and Vulnerability) as a country-level vulnerability
predictor. CIRV is itself a composite of INFORM Risk, INFORM Severity, and a
CERF-specific multi-indicator component
([Rost, Ham & Kaffes 2026](https://doi.org/10.1186/s41018-025-00184-x)).
Here we test whether replacing CIRV with the underlying INFORM indices
directly could improve or simplify the model.

We test this by substituting CIRV with **(1)** the INFORM Risk Index
(forward-looking structural risk, annual), **(2)** the INFORM Severity
Index (backward-looking crisis conditions, monthly), and **(3)** both
together. On the full 3RM dataset (n=422), INFORM Risk performs
comparably to CIRV (Adj R² = 0.709 vs 0.707). On the subset with
Severity coverage (n=237, 2019+), using both Risk and Severity together
yields the best fit (Adj R² = 0.729), with both coefficients significant.
Decomposing either index into individual pillars does not improve on
the composite scores.
:::

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| output: false
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, "..")

import ocha_stratus as stratus
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import statsmodels.api as sm
from io import BytesIO
from great_tables import GT, loc, style
from plotnine import *
import warnings
from dotenv import load_dotenv

load_dotenv()
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore", category=FutureWarning)
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore", category=UserWarning, module="plotnine")
```

## Background: INFORM Risk vs INFORM Severity

The [INFORM framework](https://drmkc.jrc.ec.europa.eu/inform-index)
provides two complementary indices, each capturing a different dimension
of humanitarian context:

| | INFORM Risk | INFORM Severity |
|---|---|---|
| **Orientation** | Forward-looking | Backward-looking |
| **Question** | "How at-risk is this country?" | "How bad is the crisis right now?" |
| **Frequency** | Annual | Monthly |
| **Unit** | Country | Country (aggregated from crisis-level) |
| **Scale** | 0--10 | 0--10 |
| **Source** | DRMKC / JRC | ACAPS / JRC |
| **Auth required** | No | Yes (ACAPS token) |

[**INFORM Risk**](https://drmkc.jrc.ec.europa.eu/inform-index/InformRisk/Methodology)
is the geometric mean of three pillars:

- **Hazard & Exposure (HA)**: natural hazards (earthquake, flood, cyclone, etc.) and human hazards (conflict)
- **Vulnerability (VU)**: socio-economic vulnerability, inequality, uprooted people
- **Lack of Coping Capacity (CC)**: institutional capacity, infrastructure, health access

[**INFORM Severity**](https://drmkc.jrc.ec.europa.eu/inform-index/INFORM-Severity/Methodology)
is a multiplicative-additive composite of three dimensions
([Poljan&scaron;ek et al. 2020](https://drmkc.jrc.ec.europa.eu/inform-index/Portals/0/InfoRM/Severity/INFORM%20Severity%20Methodology%20%2020201019%20online.pdf)):

- **Impact**: geographic and human impact of the crisis
- **Conditions of affected people**: people in need, concentration of conditions
- **Complexity**: societal safety, operating environment

The Severity composite is computed as:

$$
S = 0.70 \times \left( I^{0.33} \times C^{0.66} \right) + 0.30 \times X
$$

where $I$ = Impact, $C$ = Conditions, and $X$ = Complexity. Because Impact
and Conditions enter via geometric mean, a zero in either drives the
combined term to zero.

[**CIRV**](https://doi.org/10.1186/s41018-025-00184-x), used in the 3RM, is a
weighted average of INFORM Risk (rescaled to 0--100), INFORM Severity
(rescaled to 0--100), and a third CERF-specific multi-indicator component
covering food insecurity (IPC), early warning (IASC), and conflict dynamics
(ACLED). Each contributes one-third of the CIRV score. Before 2020, when
Severity did not yet exist, CIRV used only Risk and the multi-indicator
component (50/50 weighting).

## Data

### INFORM Risk (DRMKC API)

The INFORM Risk API (`drmkc.jrc.ec.europa.eu`) provides scores for all
countries across back-calculated years using the latest methodology. No
authentication is required.

```{python}
#| label: load-risk
#| output: false

# Fetch INFORM Risk via the Trends endpoint (all years, default workflow)
import requests

API_BASE = "https://drmkc.jrc.ec.europa.eu/inform-index/API/InformAPI"
RISK_INDICATORS = ["INFORM", "HA", "VU", "CC"]

# Get current default workflow
wf = requests.get(f"{API_BASE}/Workflows/Default", timeout=30).json()
wf_id = wf["WorkflowId"]

# Fetch all years in one call via Trends endpoint
indicator_str = ",".join(RISK_INDICATORS)
r = requests.get(
    f"{API_BASE}/Countries/Trends/",
    params={"WorkflowId": wf_id, "IndicatorId": indicator_str},
    timeout=60,
)
r.raise_for_status()
df_risk_raw = pd.DataFrame(r.json())

# Pivot to one row per country-year
df_risk = (
    df_risk_raw[["Iso3", "GNAYear", "IndicatorId", "IndicatorScore"]]
    .pivot_table(
        index=["Iso3", "GNAYear"],
        columns="IndicatorId",
        values="IndicatorScore",
        aggfunc="first",
    )
    .reset_index()
    .rename(columns={"Iso3": "iso3", "GNAYear": "year"})
)
df_risk.columns.name = None
```

```{python}
#| label: risk-summary
print(f"INFORM Risk: {len(df_risk)} rows, "
      f"{df_risk['iso3'].nunique()} countries, "
      f"years {df_risk['year'].min()}–{df_risk['year'].max()}")
print(f"Null counts: {df_risk[RISK_INDICATORS].isnull().sum().sum()}")
```

### INFORM Severity (ACAPS)

The INFORM Severity Index is available from the ACAPS API (requires
authentication) and is also stored as a pre-processed parquet file on
OCHA blob storage. We use the blob version for reproducibility and speed.

Severity is reported at the crisis level. Multiple crises can exist in a
single country; records with `country_level == "Yes"` are pre-computed
national aggregates (using max across overlapping crises per indicator,
then re-deriving the composite). For the rare country-months with multiple
`country_level == "Yes"` entries (8 countries, primarily transitional
reclassifications), we take the maximum severity score.

```{python}
#| label: load-severity
#| output: false
df_sev_raw = stratus.load_parquet_from_blob(
    "output/acaps_inform_severity/raw.parquet",
    stage="prod",
    container_name="hdx-signals",
)
df_sev_raw["date"] = pd.to_datetime(df_sev_raw["date"])

# Keep country-level, drop nulls, aggregate multi-crisis months
df_sev = (
    df_sev_raw[df_sev_raw["country_level"] == "Yes"]
    .dropna(subset=["inform_severity_index"])
    .copy()
)
df_sev["year_month"] = df_sev["date"].dt.to_period("M")
df_sev = (
    df_sev.groupby(["iso3", "year_month"])
    .agg(
        severity=("inform_severity_index", "max"),
        impact=("impact_crisis", "max"),
        conditions=("people_condition", "max"),
        complexity=("complexity", "max"),
    )
    .reset_index()
)
```

```{python}
#| label: severity-summary
date_min = df_sev_raw[df_sev_raw["country_level"] == "Yes"]["date"].min().date()
date_max = df_sev_raw[df_sev_raw["country_level"] == "Yes"]["date"].max().date()
print(f"INFORM Severity: {len(df_sev)} country-months, "
      f"{df_sev['iso3'].nunique()} countries, "
      f"{date_min} to {date_max}")

# Multi-crisis months
raw_cl = df_sev_raw[df_sev_raw["country_level"] == "Yes"]
multi = raw_cl.groupby(["iso3", "date"]).size()
n_multi = (multi > 1).sum()
print(f"Country-months with multiple crises (aggregated via max): {n_multi}")
```

### Joining INFORM Data to the 3RM Dataset

The 3RM dataset contains 422 rapid-response allocations (2016--2025), each
with a country name, year, and submission date. We join the two INFORM
datasets using different strategies:

- **INFORM Risk** is joined by **iso3 + year**, since Risk scores are annual
  and country-level. The 3RM does not include ISO3 codes, so we map country
  names manually. Risk data spans 2016--2025, matching the 3RM period exactly.
- **INFORM Severity** is joined by **iso3 + year-month**, matching each
  allocation to the Severity score for that country in the month the
  allocation was submitted. This leverages Severity's monthly resolution
  rather than aggregating to yearly values, capturing the crisis conditions
  at the time of the allocation decision. Severity data is only available
  from January 2019, so 2016--2018 allocations will have no Severity match.

For INFORM Severity, we apply two filters before joining:

1. **Country-level records only** (`country_level == "Yes"`). The raw data
   includes both country-level aggregates and individual sub-crisis records.
   The country-level record is a pre-computed national aggregate: for
   overlapping crises, ACAPS takes the maximum per indicator; for
   non-overlapping crises, it sums. The composite score is then re-derived
   from these aggregated indicators.
2. **Max severity for multi-crisis months.** In 8 countries (COL, ETH, IDN,
   IRQ, MAR, MEX, MRT, PHL), transitional reclassifications produce multiple
   `country_level == "Yes"` records in the same month. We take the maximum
   severity score, consistent with ACAPS's own aggregation approach for
   overlapping crises.

```{python}
#| label: load-3rm
#| output: false

_3RM_BLOB = (
    "ds-storm-impact-harmonisation/raw/"
    "CERF 3RM - RR Regression Model - version 1.8.xlsx"
)

# All 86 country names in 3RM MergedData -> ISO3
_COUNTRY_TO_ISO3 = {
    "Afghanistan": "AFG", "Angola": "AGO", "Antigua and Barbuda": "ATG",
    "Armenia": "ARM", "Azerbaijan": "AZE", "Bahamas": "BHS",
    "Bangladesh": "BGD", "Bolivia": "BOL", "Brazil": "BRA",
    "Burkina Faso": "BFA", "Burundi": "BDI", "Cameroon": "CMR",
    "Central African Republic": "CAF", "Chad": "TCD", "Colombia": "COL",
    "Comoros": "COM", "Cote d'Ivoire": "CIV", "Cuba": "CUB",
    "Democratic People's Republic of Korea": "PRK",
    "Democratic Republic of the Congo": "COD",
    "Djibouti": "DJI", "Dominica": "DMA", "Ecuador": "ECU",
    "Egypt": "EGY", "El Salvador": "SLV", "Equatorial Guinea": "GNQ",
    "Eritrea": "ERI", "Ethiopia": "ETH", "Fiji": "FJI",
    "Gambia": "GMB", "Guatemala": "GTM", "Guinea": "GIN",
    "Haiti": "HTI", "Honduras": "HND", "India": "IND",
    "Indonesia": "IDN", "Iraq": "IRQ",
    "Islamic Republic of Iran": "IRN", "Jamaica": "JAM",
    "Jordan": "JOR", "Kenya": "KEN", "Kyrgyzstan": "KGZ",
    "Lao People's Democratic Republic": "LAO",
    "Lebanon": "LBN", "Lesotho": "LSO", "Libya": "LBY",
    "Madagascar": "MDG", "Malawi": "MWI", "Mali": "MLI",
    "Mauritania": "MRT", "Mongolia": "MNG", "Mozambique": "MOZ",
    "Myanmar": "MMR", "Namibia": "NAM", "Nepal": "NPL",
    "Nicaragua": "NIC", "Niger": "NER", "Nigeria": "NGA",
    "Pakistan": "PAK", "Panama": "PAN", "Papua New Guinea": "PNG",
    "Peru": "PER", "Philippines": "PHL",
    "Republic of Congo": "COG", "Republic of the Sudan": "SDN",
    "Rwanda": "RWA", "Saint Vincent and the Grenadines": "VCT",
    "Samoa": "WSM", "Somalia": "SOM", "South Sudan": "SSD",
    "Sri Lanka": "LKA", "Sudan": "SDN", "Swaziland": "SWZ",
    "Syrian Arab Republic": "SYR", "Timor-Leste": "TLS",
    "Tonga": "TON", "Uganda": "UGA", "Ukraine": "UKR",
    "United Republic of Tanzania": "TZA", "Vanuatu": "VUT",
    "Venezuela": "VEN", "Viet Nam": "VNM", "Yemen": "YEM",
    "Zambia": "ZMB", "Zimbabwe": "ZWE", "oPt": "PSE",
}

raw_3rm = stratus.load_blob_data(_3RM_BLOB)
df_3rm = pd.read_excel(BytesIO(raw_3rm), sheet_name="MergedData")
df_3rm = df_3rm.rename(columns={"Application Code": "ApplicationCode"})
df_3rm["date"] = pd.to_datetime(df_3rm["Date of Most Recent Submission"])
df_3rm["iso3"] = df_3rm["Country"].map(_COUNTRY_TO_ISO3)
df_3rm["year"] = df_3rm["Year"]
df_3rm["year_month"] = df_3rm["date"].dt.to_period("M")

# Join INFORM Risk (by iso3 + year)
df = df_3rm.merge(
    df_risk.rename(columns={
        "INFORM": "inform_risk", "HA": "inform_ha",
        "VU": "inform_vu", "CC": "inform_cc",
    }),
    on=["iso3", "year"],
    how="left",
)

# Join INFORM Severity (by iso3 + year_month)
df = df.merge(
    df_sev.rename(columns={
        "severity": "inform_severity", "impact": "inform_impact",
        "conditions": "inform_conditions", "complexity": "inform_complexity",
    }),
    on=["iso3", "year_month"],
    how="left",
)

# Composite variable: mean(Risk, Severity) where both available, else Risk alone
df["inform_composite"] = np.where(
    df["inform_severity"].notna(),
    (df["inform_risk"] + df["inform_severity"]) / 2,
    df["inform_risk"],
)
```

```{python}
#| label: coverage-table
cov_rows = [
    {
        "Variable": "CIRV (Rost 2025)",
        "Matched": int(df["CIRV"].notna().sum()),
        "Missing": int(df["CIRV"].isna().sum()),
        "Coverage": f"{df['CIRV'].notna().mean():.0%}",
        "Join key": "Embedded in 3RM",
    },
    {
        "Variable": "INFORM Risk",
        "Matched": int(df["inform_risk"].notna().sum()),
        "Missing": int(df["inform_risk"].isna().sum()),
        "Coverage": f"{df['inform_risk'].notna().mean():.0%}",
        "Join key": "iso3 + year",
    },
    {
        "Variable": "INFORM Severity",
        "Matched": int(df["inform_severity"].notna().sum()),
        "Missing": int(df["inform_severity"].isna().sum()),
        "Coverage": f"{df['inform_severity'].notna().mean():.0%}",
        "Join key": "iso3 + year-month",
    },
    {
        "Variable": "INFORM Composite",
        "Matched": int(df["inform_composite"].notna().sum()),
        "Missing": int(df["inform_composite"].isna().sum()),
        "Coverage": f"{df['inform_composite'].notna().mean():.0%}",
        "Join key": "mean(Risk, Severity) or Risk alone",
    },
]

(
    GT(pd.DataFrame(cov_rows))
    .tab_header(
        title="INFORM Data Coverage in 3RM Dataset",
        subtitle=f"n = {len(df)} rapid-response allocations (2016--2025)",
    )
    .tab_style(style=style.css("font-size: 0.85em;"), locations=loc.body())
    .tab_style(style=style.css("font-size: 0.85em;"), locations=loc.column_labels())
    .tab_source_note(
        "INFORM Severity coverage is lower because the index began in 2019 "
        "(140 gaps from 2016--2018). The remaining 45 gaps are countries "
        "without ACAPS crisis tracking (7) or months before that country's "
        "Severity coverage began (38)."
    )
)
```

INFORM Risk achieves full coverage because it is annual and spans the
same period as the 3RM. INFORM Severity covers 56% of allocations; the
gaps have three causes: 140 allocations from 2016--2018 (before the index
existed), 7 allocations in countries without ACAPS crisis tracking
(Comoros, Guinea, Kyrgyzstan, Equatorial Guinea, Samoa), and 38
allocations where the country's Severity coverage had not yet started
for that specific month.

```{python}
#| label: severity-gap-by-year
no_sev = df[df["inform_severity"].isnull()]
gap_df = no_sev.groupby("year").size().reset_index(name="Missing allocations")
gap_df.columns = ["Year", "Missing allocations"]

(
    GT(gap_df)
    .tab_header(
        title="INFORM Severity Gaps by Year",
        subtitle="Allocations with no matching Severity data",
    )
    .tab_style(style=style.css("font-size: 0.85em;"), locations=loc.body())
    .tab_style(style=style.css("font-size: 0.85em;"), locations=loc.column_labels())
    .tab_source_note("INFORM Severity Index launched in 2019.")
)
```

## CIRV vs INFORM: Correlation

How do the INFORM indices relate to each other and to CIRV?

```{python}
#| label: correlation-matrix
#| fig-cap: "Pairwise Pearson correlations between CIRV and INFORM components (n = rows with all values present). CIRV correlates most strongly with INFORM Risk (r = 0.82)."
corr_vars = {
    "CIRV": "CIRV",
    "inform_risk": "Risk",
    "inform_severity": "Severity",
    "inform_ha": "HA",
    "inform_vu": "VU",
    "inform_cc": "CC",
}

df_corr = df[list(corr_vars.keys())].rename(columns=corr_vars).dropna()
corr_mat = df_corr.corr()

# Melt for heatmap
corr_melt = corr_mat.reset_index().melt(id_vars="index", var_name="var2", value_name="r")
corr_melt = corr_melt.rename(columns={"index": "var1"})
var_order = list(corr_vars.values())
corr_melt["var1"] = pd.Categorical(corr_melt["var1"], categories=var_order, ordered=True)
corr_melt["var2"] = pd.Categorical(corr_melt["var2"], categories=var_order[::-1], ordered=True)

(
    ggplot(corr_melt, aes("var1", "var2", fill="r"))
    + geom_tile(color="white", size=1)
    + geom_text(aes(label="r"), format_string="{:.2f}", size=10, color="black")
    + scale_fill_gradient2(
        low="#2166ac", mid="#f7f7f7", high="#b2182b",
        midpoint=0, limits=(-1, 1), name="Pearson r",
    )
    + labs(x="", y="")
    + theme_minimal()
    + theme(
        figure_size=(7, 6),
        axis_text=element_text(size=11),
        legend_position="right",
    )
)
```

```{python}
#| label: scatter-cirv-vs-inform
#| fig-cap: "CIRV vs INFORM Risk (left) and INFORM Severity (right) for allocations with all three indices available. Both show strong positive relationships; Severity has more scatter due to its finer monthly resolution."
df_scatter = df.dropna(subset=["CIRV", "inform_risk", "inform_severity"]).copy()

scatter_long = pd.concat([
    df_scatter[["CIRV", "inform_risk"]].rename(
        columns={"inform_risk": "INFORM_score"}
    ).assign(index_type="INFORM Risk"),
    df_scatter[["CIRV", "inform_severity"]].rename(
        columns={"inform_severity": "INFORM_score"}
    ).assign(index_type="INFORM Severity"),
])

(
    ggplot(scatter_long, aes("INFORM_score", "CIRV"))
    + geom_point(alpha=0.35, size=2, color="#2166ac")
    + geom_smooth(method="lm", color="#d6604d", se=True, size=0.8)
    + facet_wrap("~index_type", scales="free_x")
    + labs(x="INFORM Score (0--10)", y="CIRV (0--100)")
    + theme_minimal()
    + theme(
        figure_size=(12, 5),
        strip_text=element_text(size=11, weight="bold"),
    )
)
```

## Regression: Replacing CIRV with INFORM

We replicate the 3RM Model 1 specification (emergency type dummies +
vulnerability index + LogRequired + LogTargeted) and compare model fit
when swapping CIRV for different INFORM alternatives.

```{python}
#| label: model-helpers
#| output: false

ETYPE_COLS = [
    "Storm", "Flood", "Drought", "OtherNatural",
    "Cholera", "Ebola", "OtherHealth", "DisplConfl",
]

def _stars(p):
    if p < 0.01:
        return "***"
    if p < 0.05:
        return "**"
    if p < 0.1:
        return "*"
    return ""

def _fmt_coef(model, var):
    """Format as coefficient with stars + p-value in parentheses."""
    b = model.params[var]
    p = model.pvalues[var]
    return f"{b:.3f}{_stars(p)} ({p:.2f})"

def fit_3rm(data, vuln_vars):
    """Fit 3RM-style OLS with given vulnerability variables."""
    regressors = ETYPE_COLS + vuln_vars + ["LogRequired", "LogTargeted"]
    sub = data.dropna(subset=regressors + ["LogApproved"])
    X = sm.add_constant(sub[regressors].astype(float))
    return sm.OLS(sub["LogApproved"], X).fit()
```

### Full Dataset (n = 422)

All 3RM allocations have both CIRV and INFORM Risk coverage, allowing
direct comparison on the same rows.

```{python}
#| label: models-full-dataset

m_cirv = fit_3rm(df, ["CIRV"])
m_risk = fit_3rm(df, ["inform_risk"])
m_composite = fit_3rm(df, ["inform_composite"])
m_vu_cc = fit_3rm(df, ["inform_vu", "inform_cc"])
m_ha_vu_cc = fit_3rm(df, ["inform_ha", "inform_vu", "inform_cc"])

# Build comparison table
var_labels = {
    "const": "Intercept",
    "Storm": "Storm", "Flood": "Flood", "Drought": "Drought",
    "OtherNatural": "Other Natural", "Cholera": "Cholera",
    "Ebola": "Ebola", "OtherHealth": "Other Health",
    "DisplConfl": "Displacement/Conflict",
    "CIRV": "CIRV", "inform_risk": "INFORM Risk",
    "inform_composite": "INFORM Composite",
    "inform_vu": "Vulnerability (VU)", "inform_cc": "Coping Capacity (CC)",
    "inform_ha": "Hazard & Exposure (HA)",
    "LogRequired": "Funding req. (ln)", "LogTargeted": "People targeted (ln)",
}

models_full = {
    "CIRV": m_cirv,
    "Risk": m_risk,
    "Composite": m_composite,
    "VU + CC": m_vu_cc,
    "HA + VU + CC": m_ha_vu_cc,
}

# All variables across models
all_vars = []
for m in models_full.values():
    for v in m.params.index:
        if v not in all_vars:
            all_vars.append(v)

tbl_rows = []
for var in all_vars:
    row = {"Variable": var_labels.get(var, var)}
    for mname, m in models_full.items():
        if var in m.params.index:
            row[mname] = _fmt_coef(m, var)
        else:
            row[mname] = ""
    tbl_rows.append(row)

tbl_rows.append({
    "Variable": "n",
    **{mname: str(int(m.nobs)) for mname, m in models_full.items()},
})
tbl_rows.append({
    "Variable": "Adj R\u00b2",
    **{mname: f"{m.rsquared_adj:.3f}" for mname, m in models_full.items()},
})
tbl_rows.append({
    "Variable": "AIC",
    **{mname: f"{m.aic:.1f}" for mname, m in models_full.items()},
})

tbl_df = pd.DataFrame(tbl_rows)

# Highlight the vulnerability/INFORM rows
inform_labels = {"CIRV", "INFORM Risk", "INFORM Composite",
                 "Vulnerability (VU)", "Coping Capacity (CC)",
                 "Hazard & Exposure (HA)"}
highlight_idx = tbl_df.index[tbl_df["Variable"].isin(inform_labels)].tolist()

(
    GT(tbl_df)
    .tab_header(
        title="3RM Model with CIRV vs INFORM Risk Alternatives",
        subtitle="Full dataset (n = 422, 2016--2025)",
    )
    .tab_style(style=style.fill(color="#e6eeff"), locations=loc.body(rows=highlight_idx))
    .tab_style(style=style.css("font-size: 0.85em;"), locations=loc.body())
    .tab_style(style=style.css("font-size: 0.85em;"), locations=loc.column_labels())
    .tab_source_note(
        "Outcome: ln(CERF allocation). Base emergency type: 'Other'. "
        "Blue rows = vulnerability/risk component being tested. "
        "*** p < 0.01, ** p < 0.05, * p < 0.1."
    )
)
```

INFORM Risk achieves marginally better Adj R² (0.709) and AIC (565) than
CIRV (0.707, AIC 569) on the same data. The INFORM Composite -- defined
as mean(Risk, Severity) where Severity is available, Risk alone otherwise
-- slightly underperforms Risk alone (0.705 vs 0.709) because the single
coefficient must reconcile pure-Risk rows (pre-2019) with blended rows
(2019+). Decomposing Risk into individual pillars does not improve the
model: VU + CC alone (0.699) is worse, and adding HA back recovers the
same fit but with more parameters.

### Severity Subset (n = 237, 2019+)

To compare Severity models fairly, we restrict all models to the 237
allocations with Severity coverage.

```{python}
#| label: models-severity-subset

df_sub = df.dropna(subset=["inform_severity"]).copy()

m_cirv_s = fit_3rm(df_sub, ["CIRV"])
m_risk_s = fit_3rm(df_sub, ["inform_risk"])
m_sev_s = fit_3rm(df_sub, ["inform_severity"])
m_sev_pillars = fit_3rm(
    df_sub, ["inform_impact", "inform_conditions", "inform_complexity"]
)
m_both = fit_3rm(df_sub, ["inform_risk", "inform_severity"])
m_composite_s = fit_3rm(df_sub, ["inform_composite"])

var_labels_sub = {
    **var_labels,
    "inform_severity": "INFORM Severity",
    "inform_impact": "Impact",
    "inform_conditions": "Conditions",
    "inform_complexity": "Complexity",
}

models_sub = {
    "CIRV": m_cirv_s,
    "Risk": m_risk_s,
    "Severity": m_sev_s,
    "Composite": m_composite_s,
    "Sev. pillars": m_sev_pillars,
    "Risk + Sev.": m_both,
}

all_vars_sub = []
for m in models_sub.values():
    for v in m.params.index:
        if v not in all_vars_sub:
            all_vars_sub.append(v)

tbl_rows_sub = []
for var in all_vars_sub:
    row = {"Variable": var_labels_sub.get(var, var)}
    for mname, m in models_sub.items():
        if var in m.params.index:
            row[mname] = _fmt_coef(m, var)
        else:
            row[mname] = ""
    tbl_rows_sub.append(row)

tbl_rows_sub.append({
    "Variable": "n",
    **{mname: str(int(m.nobs)) for mname, m in models_sub.items()},
})
tbl_rows_sub.append({
    "Variable": "Adj R\u00b2",
    **{mname: f"{m.rsquared_adj:.3f}" for mname, m in models_sub.items()},
})
tbl_rows_sub.append({
    "Variable": "AIC",
    **{mname: f"{m.aic:.1f}" for mname, m in models_sub.items()},
})

tbl_df_sub = pd.DataFrame(tbl_rows_sub)

inform_labels_sub = {
    "CIRV", "INFORM Risk", "INFORM Severity", "INFORM Composite",
    "Impact", "Conditions", "Complexity",
}
highlight_idx_sub = tbl_df_sub.index[
    tbl_df_sub["Variable"].isin(inform_labels_sub)
].tolist()

(
    GT(tbl_df_sub)
    .tab_header(
        title="3RM Model with CIRV vs INFORM Alternatives",
        subtitle="Severity subset (n = 237, 2019+) for apples-to-apples comparison",
    )
    .tab_style(
        style=style.fill(color="#e6eeff"),
        locations=loc.body(rows=highlight_idx_sub),
    )
    .tab_style(style=style.css("font-size: 0.85em;"), locations=loc.body())
    .tab_style(style=style.css("font-size: 0.85em;"), locations=loc.column_labels())
    .tab_source_note(
        "Outcome: ln(CERF allocation). All models use the same 237 rows. "
        "Blue rows = vulnerability/risk component being tested. "
        "*** p < 0.01, ** p < 0.05, * p < 0.1."
    )
)
```

### Summary

```{python}
#| label: summary-comparison
#| fig-cap: "Adjusted R² for each model specification. Models available on the full dataset (n=422) shown in blue; models requiring the severity subset (n=237, 2019+) in orange. Dashed line marks the CIRV baseline on the severity subset."

summary_rows = [
    {"Model": "Risk: VU + CC", "Adj R²": m_vu_cc.rsquared_adj,
     "Dataset": "Full (n=422)"},
    {"Model": "CIRV", "Adj R²": m_cirv.rsquared_adj,
     "Dataset": "Full (n=422)"},
    {"Model": "INFORM Risk", "Adj R²": m_risk.rsquared_adj,
     "Dataset": "Full (n=422)"},
    {"Model": "Risk: HA+VU+CC", "Adj R²": m_ha_vu_cc.rsquared_adj,
     "Dataset": "Full (n=422)"},
    {"Model": "INFORM Composite", "Adj R²": m_composite.rsquared_adj,
     "Dataset": "Full (n=422)"},
    {"Model": "CIRV*", "Adj R²": m_cirv_s.rsquared_adj,
     "Dataset": "Subset (n=237)"},
    {"Model": "INFORM Composite*", "Adj R²": m_composite_s.rsquared_adj,
     "Dataset": "Subset (n=237)"},
    {"Model": "Severity pillars", "Adj R²": m_sev_pillars.rsquared_adj,
     "Dataset": "Subset (n=237)"},
    {"Model": "INFORM Risk*", "Adj R²": m_risk_s.rsquared_adj,
     "Dataset": "Subset (n=237)"},
    {"Model": "INFORM Severity", "Adj R²": m_sev_s.rsquared_adj,
     "Dataset": "Subset (n=237)"},
    {"Model": "Risk + Severity", "Adj R²": m_both.rsquared_adj,
     "Dataset": "Subset (n=237)"},
]
summary_df = pd.DataFrame(summary_rows)
summary_df["Model"] = pd.Categorical(
    summary_df["Model"],
    categories=summary_df["Model"].tolist(),
    ordered=True,
)

(
    ggplot(summary_df, aes("Adj R²", "Model", color="Dataset"))
    + geom_vline(
        xintercept=m_cirv_s.rsquared_adj,
        linetype="dashed", color="#999999", size=0.5,
    )
    + geom_segment(aes(x=0.695, xend="Adj R²", yend="Model"), color="#dddddd", size=0.5)
    + geom_point(size=4)
    + geom_text(
        aes(label="Adj R²"), format_string="{:.3f}",
        nudge_x=0.0015, size=9, ha="left", show_legend=False,
    )
    + scale_color_manual(values={"Full (n=422)": "#2166ac", "Subset (n=237)": "#d6604d"})
    + scale_x_continuous(limits=(0.695, 0.735))
    + labs(x="Adjusted R²", y="", color="")
    + theme_minimal()
    + theme(
        figure_size=(10, 5),
        axis_text_y=element_text(size=10),
        legend_position="top",
    )
)
```

## Interpretation

- **INFORM Risk is a drop-in replacement for CIRV.** On the full dataset
  (n=422), swapping CIRV for INFORM Risk produces nearly identical fit
  (Adj R² 0.709 vs 0.707, lower AIC). This is unsurprising given CIRV is
  partly derived from INFORM Risk.
- **Both INFORM indices individually outperform CIRV.** On the same 237
  rows, INFORM Risk and Severity each achieve Adj R² = 0.724 vs 0.717
  for CIRV.
- **Risk + Severity together is the best specification** (Adj R² = 0.729,
  lowest AIC). Both coefficients are significant (p < 0.03), capturing
  non-redundant information: structural vulnerability (Risk) and current
  crisis conditions (Severity).
- **Decomposing into pillars does not help.** The composite scores are
  more efficient predictors than their constituent pillars. For Severity,
  only Complexity is individually significant, likely because Impact and
  Conditions enter the composite multiplicatively and are hard to
  disentangle in a linear model.
- **Severity coverage constrains the comparison.** The 237-row subset
  excludes 2016--2018. For full temporal coverage, INFORM Risk alone
  (n=422, Adj R² = 0.709) is the practical choice.
- **The INFORM Composite offers limited advantage.** On the 2019+
  subset, the composite (single variable) matches the two-variable
  Risk + Severity model (0.730 vs 0.729), as expected given the near-equal
  coefficients. On the full dataset, however, the composite (0.705) is
  slightly worse than Risk alone (0.709): the single coefficient must
  compromise between pure-Risk rows (pre-2019) and blended rows (2019+),
  introducing inconsistency.

## Key Findings

- **INFORM Risk can replace CIRV** with no loss in fit and the advantage
  of being a transparent, publicly available index (no authentication,
  open API).
- **Adding INFORM Severity** produces the best model (Adj R² = 0.729),
  but requires an ACAPS token and is limited to 2019+.
- **The INFORM Composite** (mean of Risk and Severity, falling back to
  Risk alone pre-2019) matches the two-variable model on 2019+ data but
  slightly underperforms Risk alone on the full dataset. It does not
  offer a clear advantage over simpler alternatives.
- **Composite scores outperform individual pillars** for both indices.
- **Operational recommendation:** given the modest Adj R² improvement
  from Severity (0.724 vs 0.709) and the benefit of fitting on the
  larger 2016+ dataset (n=422 vs 237), INFORM Risk alone is the
  recommended specification.
